items.title: Caddie: researched LOCAL-LORE layer on hole guides (green character, history, architect intent)
items.why: OWNER GAP REPORT 2026-07-19 (Pinehurst No.2 first-green ChatGPT screenshots): ChatGPT surfaces researched LORE (false front that sheds balls, 2024 U.S. Open pins on 2-4% slopes, Donald Ross turtleback greens falling away, 'landing short is dead', 'safest play: center of the green below the hole') the caddie CANNOT say because the payload carries only geometry-provable facts. CONTENT gap. Build: (1) ADDITIVE `local_lore` section on HoleStrategyGuide (JSONB additive key, NO migration) — green-complex character, famous features/names, play-relevant tournament history, architect intent; each item ATTRIBUTED + register-matched. (2) guide_writer lore research pass (web_search) with a DIFFERENT validation bar than geometry claims: lore may add non-geometric knowledge but must NOT contradict our proven geometry; no unattributed superlatives; confidence-gate proper nouns (course_intel_writer pattern). Existing tactical-section validation stays BYTE-IDENTICAL. (3) strategy payload/brain: local_lore in the local-knowledge block (labeled 'researched local knowledge — attributed'); _strategy_system may SPEAK lore for green-character/how-to-play questions while numbers stay engine-bound; read-time verdict gate governs tactical claims (lore rides if guide passes). (4) ADDITIVE backfill runner over already-guided courses (append lore, never regenerate tactical). Acceptance: offline Pinehurst-1-shaped guide WITH lore -> payload carries false-front/below-the-hole knowledge + a synth stub speaking it passes validation; a geometry-contradicting lore item -> rejected. Surface: guide_writer + guide schema + strategy payload assembly. Parallel lanes: persona-consistency (prompt registers), flip-prep (authz) — coordinate/rebase.

items.title: Flip the text-advice caddie mouths from Sonnet 4.5 to Sonnet 5 (gated on a live before/after eval)
items.why: Lever (a) from the 2026-07-17 caddie model audit. The text advice mouths (/caddie/session/voice + stream, /voice + stream) run claude-sonnet-4-5-20250929 via tool_loop.py. The enabling change LANDED this cycle (caddie-advice-model-decoupling): dedicated CADDIE_ADVICE_MODEL env + conditional temperature, so flipping to Sonnet 5 is now a ONE-ENV change (CADDIE_ADVICE_MODEL=claude-sonnet-5) without the 400 the shared ANTHROPIC_MODEL would have caused and without touching memory/OCR/voice. WHAT REMAINS before the flip ships: (1) a LIVE before/after eval — the consistency probe (tests/eval/run_consistency.py) + the tier2 conversation goldens (tests/eval/run_tier2.py) run on both sonnet-4-5 and sonnet-5 candidates, with measured pass counts (needs ANTHROPIC_API_KEY + bounded spend; NOT available in the eng-lead worktree — run in CI or a keyed env). HONESTY RULE: no flip without measured before/after. (2) update the eval runners: run_consistency.py + run_tier2.py hard-code candidate temperature=0.7 and default the candidate to ANTHROPIC_MODEL — add the same conditional-temperature gate + a CADDIE_ADVICE_MODEL default before evaluating a Sonnet-5 candidate. (3) consider thinking={type:disabled} on the sonnet-5 branch in tool_loop.py (Sonnet 5 runs adaptive thinking by default; max_tokens=300 is a TOTAL cap so thinking could truncate the spoken reply) — the decoupling builder was asked to note this; confirm whether it was added or is still a flip-day rider. NOTE the owner's crux is the VOICE orb (gpt-realtime), not this text path — so caddie-smart-strategy-tool is the higher-impact lever; this flip is a modest, cheap-during-intro ($2/$10 vs $3/$15) quality bump worth doing once proven. Intro pricing window ends 2026-08-31.

items.title: Per-hole satellite 'visual read' — vision-model grounding at hole-open (feasibility spike, then build)
items.why: Lever (c) from the 2026-07-17 caddie model audit — the owner's screenshot insight: the caddie has never SEEN the hole, it only gets polygons, so it misses things like 'trees left' that OSM ingest lacks. DESIGN (spike-then-build): at guide-precompute time (the same job as guide_writer.py) fetch a static satellite tile for the hole's bbox (centerline/tee/green already mapped in PostGIS — overlay them as markers), send to Sonnet 5 vision (high-res ~2576px; ~1.6-4.8K image tokens) together with build_ground_truth_block, ask for a QUALITATIVE visual read only: tree lines left/right, visual fairway width, bail-out side, unmapped waste/bunker shapes. HARD RULE: no yardages — numbers stay with the engine; validate/reject like validate_guide does for text guides. Store as visual_read next to strategy_guide on hole intel; fold into the caddie-smart-strategy-tool payload (and optionally the prompt guide line). COST ~=$0.01-0.02/hole intro pricing, one-time per course (<= ~$0.40/course). LATENCY: none live (precompute + cache like guides); ~3-5s on-demand fallback at a hole-open cache miss. WHAT IT CATCHES: the owner's exact 'trees left' case on holes where OSM has no tree data (partial tree ingest exists — test_tree_hazards.py — so first cross-check coverage). SPIKE OPEN QUESTIONS (do before building): tile source + LICENSING (Mapbox Static vs Google Static), imagery recency, server-side marker-overlay rendering, hallucination rate on low-res tiles. Sequence AFTER caddie-smart-strategy-tool (it feeds that tool's payload).

items.title: Aim reticle: subtle grab affordance on drag-start (discoverability polish)

The reticle is now draggable but keeps the existing tap-target.png art with no distinct 'grab' cue; designer APPROVED ship-as-is (tap-to-place is the fully-discoverable primary path) but suggested a subtle 'lift' cue to improve drag discoverability: on drag-start bump the reticle iconSize/anchor ~15-20% (e.g. 38->44px, anchor 19->22) via a marker replace and revert on drag-end — uses the EXISTING asset, no new art, no new color language, no Material drag-handle. Revisit if TestFlight feedback says the drag isn't discovered.

items.title: Yardage plates (200/150/100) balloon at close zoom — use a fixed screen-space dot, not a ground-radius Circle
items.why: Flagged during the lettered-bunker-legend lane (owner v1.1.8 screenshot item-4 check). The 200/150/100 plates render in GoogleSatelliteMap.tsx addTeeShotOverlays as native Circles with radius:4 METERS (ground-anchored), so they visually BALLOON in screen px at close zoom — the 'large plain circles' the owner's screenshot showed. NOT a zoom artifact of the letter change and NOT a new regression: it is the shipped plate implementation's inherent behavior (fixed ground radius). VERDICT (designer + fable both): a distance plate marks a discrete yardage STATION, not a real-world-sized object — it should read as a fixed small SCREEN-space dot regardless of zoom (icon-marker idiom, like the bunker/tee glyphs, ~10-12px), not a fixed-meter circle. This is a Circle→icon MECHANISM swap: needs a plate glyph asset (or per-yardage colored dot PNGs matching PLATE_FILL_BY_YARDS), rework of the circles block + its id-tracking/removal, and re-verification that plate placement/visibility math is untouched. Medium effort, deserves its own fable plan + review. Deliberately deferred out of the letter lane to keep that change scoped. FILES: frontend/src/components/GoogleSatelliteMap.tsx (addTeeShotOverlays circles block, PLATE_FILL_BY_YARDS, teeShotCircleIdsRef).

items.title: EPIC: Multi-user Looper — secure accounts, profiles/connect, Virtual Match (App Store)
items.p0_status_note: P0 FOUNDATION CODE-COMPLETE 2026-07-16 (all on integration/next, SILENT/dark — APP_ACCESS_MODE stays owner-default so the owner's app is byte-identical; NOTHING flipped yet). The four ready P0 slices all landed with reviewer SHIP + qa PASS: slice 1 multiuser-p0-authz-flip (require_member foundation, bundle #142); slice 2 multiuser-p0-client-identity (useMe + per-user localStorage namespacing, bundle #143); slice 3 multiuser-p0-migrations-revocation (Svix-verified Clerk webhook + revocation store + AUTH_BYPASS prod-build CI guard; migrations DESIGNED-only, bundle #144); slice 4 multiuser-p0-keychain-token (native JWT -> iOS Keychain @59e899b, bundle #144). REMAINING P0 EXIT CRITERIA — all FLIP-TIME / owner-gated, NOT unattended loop work: (a) run the three guarded Alembic migrations (backfill owner_id / tighten NOT NULL after soak / revoked_users CREATE) as their own reviewed PR at the flip window; (b) multiuser-p0-signout-namespace-clear (LOW, close before open flip); (c) multiuser-p0-self-savedplayer (needs the small PlayerCreate.clerkUserId server change); (d) device/sim acceptance of the Keychain migrate-from-plaintext + fresh Clerk sign-in (XCUITest, attended); (e) the two clerk-jwt-keychain-swap LOW followups (sign-out TOCTOU + cold-start stale-token clear). P1 (multiuser-p1-profiles-discovery-connect) is next but OWNER-GATED on soak/launch sequencing + the OWNER-DECISION list in the spec (Clerk plan, phone-SMS costs, discoverability default, App Store account-deletion, RLS-now-or-later, launch ramp) — do NOT start P1 until the owner sequences the flip.
items.why: Owner greenlit 2026-07-16 (verbatim): 'make this multi user... distributed on the App Store... a very secure login system, and ability to connect with users and search their profiles connect via phone number, email... Then I want to implement that virtual match capability.' Phased, security-first program in specs/multi-user-epic-plan.md (Plan agent on fable + grounded auth audit + PM P1/P2 shapes + reviewer P0 threat-model). KEY FINDINGS: backend is ALREADY multi-tenant-shaped (row scoping by verified Clerk sub is pervasive; require_owner is a single env-var gate at main.py:62 over 17 routers) so 'the flip' is small; the real work is closing 3 unscoped-route gaps (pins/job-ids/personas), an authz-isolation test suite, and the CLIENT (derive 'me' from Clerk, per-user localStorage namespacing, Keychain token). RLS DECISION: app-layer scoping for P0 + a user_session() centralization that makes RLS a drop-in later hardening (deferred to end of P1). This is the parent tracker; buildable work = the multiuser-p0-* slices below. See the OWNER-DECISION list in the spec (Clerk plan/pricing, phone-SMS costs, discoverability default opt-in, migration timing, App Store account-deletion, hole_pins per-user, RLS-now-or-later, LLM cost per stranger, launch ramp).

items.title: P0: per-user authz flip (require_member) + close unscoped-route gaps + isolation test suite
items.why: specs/multi-user-epic-plan.md §3.1/§3.3/§3.6 — the FIRST buildable P0 slice. (1) Add APP_ACCESS_MODE env (owner|open) in clerk_auth.py + a require_member dep: owner-mode = byte-identical to today's require_owner; open-mode = any verified Clerk sub. Swap main.py:62 _owner_only -> _member on all 17 routers; keep require_owner on the by-nature owner-only endpoints (caller-voice/rehearsal-call, tee_times.py:633/767/787). (2) Close the gaps the flip exposes: [BLOCKING, reviewer pass] carve courses_mapped.py POST/PUT/DELETE (:68/:109/:126 — member-writable GLOBAL course geometry, no owner column; PUT/DELETE re-precompute elevations+guides so a stranger corrupts every user's caddie yardages) to STAY require_owner post-flip + negative write-authz test; hole_pins -> per-user (add user_id, unique key (course,hole,date,user_id), scope pins.py list/upsert — recommended, protects caddie yardage integrity; owner decision #6); stamp+match user_id on the _availability_jobs dict + any future async course-search job (uuid is not authz; NOTE scorecard /scan is synchronous, no job-id — and request_availability_call must stay owner-only OR require the member's own callbackNumber, never fall back to the owner's number); caddie_personas read=is_builtin|is_public|author==me, write/delete author-scoped 404. (3) Introduce a canonical user_session(user_id) acquisition helper every tenant route migrates to (no behavior change now; the single seam RLS plugs into later) + a CI scoping-lint. (4) The AUTHZ-ISOLATION TEST SUITE is the acceptance bar: parametrized per-router A-creates/B-gets-404, LIST excludes, mutate-404-and-unchanged; 404-not-403 enumeration property identical to random-nonexistent-id; flip-regression (owner-mode 403s non-owner, open-mode both pass). Harness already supports identity injection (tests/integration/conftest.py:176-184). MAJOR/auth -> /security-review + /code-review MANDATORY before ready. Single-user stays byte-identical (owner mode default). No migration RUN in this slice (design only).

items.title: P0: self-SavedPlayer(clerkUserId=me) + round-new ownerIndex default (backend+client)
items.why: specs/multi-user-epic-plan.md §3.5 pieces (1b)+(2), deferred from multiuser-p0-client-identity because PlayerCreate/POST /api/players (backend/app/models.py:61-74) have no clerkUserId field to stamp, so a client-only self-player create would mint duplicate 'Me' rows per device/sign-in. Scope: (a) backend — accept optional clerkUserId in PlayerCreate, server-VALIDATE it == current_user_id (never trust the client value), unique-per-owner so it's idempotent; keep types.ts<->models.py in sync. (b) client — useMe() ensures exactly one self SavedPlayer with clerkUserId=me exists on sign-in (idempotent); round-new defaults ownerIndex to the roster player whose clerkUserId===me (pill stays a rare override, no visual change). Byte-identical for the owner. Isolation test: two accounts each get their own single self-player, never cross-linked.

items.title: P0 hardening: clear scorecard_last_user_id + current namespace on Clerk signOut (pre-open-flip)
items.why: security-review LOW finding on multiuser-p0-client-identity (2026-07-16): nothing clears scorecard_last_user_id on sign-out, so on native (where window.Clerk.user.id often never hydrates) a same-device account switch can transiently resolve pref-store reads (persona/favorites/map-view) to the PRIOR user's namespace for one render before IdentityBridge's effect reconciles. No server data crosses tenants (API path is current_user_id-scoped) and it self-corrects, so NOT blocking for the dark slice — but MUST close before APP_ACCESS_MODE=open. Fix: on Clerk signOut clear scorecard_last_user_id (and optionally the current namespace), and/or gate pref-store reads on useMe().isLoaded. Test: signOut then a synchronous pref read returns the default/empty, never the prior user's value.

items.title: P1: profiles + privacy-first discovery (hashed phone/email) + connect/blocks
items.why: specs/multi-user-epic-plan.md §4 (+ PM product shape). BLOCKED until the P0 isolation foundation ships. Extend golfer_profiles (handle, avatar_url, discoverable_by_email/phone default OFF, public_fields, profileVisibility). user_identifiers lookup index = HMAC-SHA256(normalized identifier, server pepper in Secrets Manager) — raw identifiers NEVER stored/queryable; rows written ONLY from Clerk webhooks (verified identifiers). user_connections (request/accept, mutual, unordered-pair unique) + user_blocks (silent, total, severs). Formalize name-string rosters via players.clerk_user_id ('Link to @mike' not a duplicate row). API: POST /users/lookup (exact handle/email/phone, no list/prefix, per-user + per-IP quota, blocked==nonexistent), connections/blocks CRUD, GET /users/{id} connected-only-or-404. Partners hub evolves /players in place (NO new bottom-tab — real nav is Home/Courses/Tee-times/Profile). App Store: native per-contact picker (no blanket Contacts perm), privacy-label update, account-deletion cascade covers identifiers/connections/blocks. Owner decisions #1/#2/#3/#8/#12/#13.

items.title: P2: Virtual Match MVP — async net match-play round-vs-round with live standings
items.why: specs/multi-user-epic-plan.md §5 (+ PM MVP shape) — the owner's headline ask. BLOCKED on P1 (needs the connection graph). MVP = two CONNECTED users each play their OWN round (existing scoring untouched) on a locked course/tee; a virtual_matches overlay computes NET match-play with a live/near-live standings feed. GROUNDING: computeMatchPlay (games.ts:739) is GROSS-only today — net allocation via HoleInfo.handicap is GENUINELY NEW logic (new pure lib/virtual-match.ts + a Python backend twin with parity tests vs games.ts fixtures, since the client can't read the opponent's raw round). Schema: virtual_matches (creator/opponent, status, per-side round_ids, locked course/tee, frozen_handicaps snapshot, result, expires_at) + optional Round.virtualMatchId mirroring tournamentId. State machine proposed->accepted->active->completed (+declined/cancelled/expired); consent structural (nothing visible pre-accept; attach-only-own-round); expiry void unless zero-hole no-show (owner decision #11). ANTI-TAMPER: neither can touch the other's scores (falls out of P0); opponent sees a minimized scores-only projection (never GPS/caddie/memories). Client: 'Challenge' on a Partner profile + quiet match card + result screen (reuse leaderboard/SettleUp visual language, NOT a new tab); caddie answers 'how's my match with Mike' as DATA not prompt-trusted text. DEFERRED: simultaneous/live, groups/leagues, money settlement, chat, rematches. /security-review + designer NORTHSTAR review. Cycle count ~3-4.

items.title: Omnipresent caddie orb discards the user's chosen persona (always 'classic')
items.note: Narrow orb-persona-threading fix was ALREADY shipped (commit 9df28c9). The caddie-crux CONSISTENCY dimension remained: the caddie's REGISTER drifted across its mouths. Landed on the bundle @98c4b90 (2026-07-19) as NOTICEABLE: one shared CADDIE_HOUSE_REGISTER constant adopted across the realtime/text/strategy-brain/guide mouths, per-persona brevity restatements pruned (Hype exempt by design), minor DECADE/slope wording align, + a thin offline register eval (test_caddie_register_consistency.py). items.why: CADDIE-EXPERIENCE dim 8 (well-integrated), the single highest-impact incoherence from the 2026-07-15 integration audit. CaddieOrbSheet.tsx hardcodes personality_id:'classic' in both talkToCaddieStream (:202) and the talkToCaddie fallback (:215), and the TTS voice is hardcoded 'classic' at LooperSheet.tsx:109. The backend /caddie/voice route fully honors personality_id, so this is a pure frontend omission. Effect: a golfer who picks The Hype Man / The Strategist on the round page gets that persona on-course, but the moment they tap the ever-present orb on Home / Partners / My Card they SILENTLY get The Classic Caddie in a different voice. Also affects the My Card converse lane (same path). FIX: consume useCaddiePersona() (the round-page standard) in CaddieOrbSheet and thread personaId into both API calls and the TTS speak()/tts.speak calls. The most visible cross-surface identity break.

items.title: Frontend persona picker lists personas with no backend counterpart (dead selections)
items.why: Flagged by the designer during caddie-orb-persona-consistency (register audit, 2026-07-19). frontend/src/lib/caddie/personalities.ts ships personas (e.g. veteran-looper, hard-edge, and others) that have NO row in backend/app/caddie/personalities.py (only strategist/classic/hype/professor exist there) nor in frontend persona.ts (which lists just the 4). A user could select a client-only persona and get silently remapped (load_personality falls back to 'classic'). This is a data-INVENTORY/integration bug, NOT register wording — deliberately kept out of the register-unification cycle. FIX (a design decision): either build the missing backend personas (new registers TBD) or delete the dead client-only entries. Verify the actual list of orphans first (persona.ts vs personalities.ts vs backend personalities.py vs the caddie_personas DB seed).

items.title: Verify prod caddie_personas rows carry no invalid Realtime voice_id (complete the fable fix in prod)
items.why: CADDIE-EXPERIENCE dim 6 (reliability). Cycle 127 fixed The Professor's invalid voice_id 'fable' -> 'cedar' in the CODE fallback (personalities.py). But personas live in the caddie_personas Postgres table (migration 003); personalities.py is only the SSR/dev fallback. If a seeded prod row still carries voice_id='fable' (or any non-Realtime voice), The Professor's LIVE realtime session still errors in prod despite the code fix. ACTION: audit caddie_personas.voice_id against the valid Realtime set {alloy,ash,ballad,coral,echo,sage,shimmer,verse,marin,cedar} and repoint any invalid row (fable->cedar). Owner/DB-gated (needs a prod DB write; the looper/prod Secrets Manager DATABASE_URL is also stale per prod-database-url-secret-refresh). Low effort once DB access is available.

items.title: OWNER A/B by ear: default caddie voice sage->cedar + speed 1.15->~1.0 (less robotic)
items.why: CADDIE-EXPERIENCE dim 4 (non-robotic voice). Voice research (2026-07-15): the current default caddie voice is 'sage' at speed 1.15; OpenAI's newer Realtime voices 'cedar' (natural/conversational) and 'marin' (professional/clear) are the biggest natural-speech improvement and are a PURE one-field session-config swap with NO latency or cost penalty (realtime_relay.py OPENAI_REALTIME_DEFAULT_VOICE + audio.output.speed). Also: speed 1.15 pushes faster than natural; ~1.0 + a 'relaxed conversational pace' instruction reads less robotic. This changes what the owner HEARS on the main (Classic) caddie, so it is owner-gated and audible-on-TestFlight only. ACTION: build a TestFlight build that swaps Classic default sage->cedar (and optionally nudges speed) so the owner judges by ear; keep behind the personality config. Deferred from cycle 127 (which landed only the correctness fable fix). NOTICEABLE.

items.title: Custom persona name input is uncapped -> long names truncate in tiny orb captions
items.why: Designer fast-follow from cycle 131 (caddie-cross-surface-identity-label). CustomPersonaModal.tsx's name <input> has no maxLength and its placeholder ('e.g. The Quiet Reader') invites long names, so a golfer can create a custom persona whose name overflows the tiny mono attribution captions in the Looper sheet. Cycle 131 already made the caption truncation word-boundary-aware (captionPersonaName in persona.ts, 'Sunday Money...' not mid-word), which fully avoids the ugly render; this is the belt-and-suspenders upstream cap. ACTION: add maxLength ~24-28 to the CustomPersonaModal name input (frontend/src/components/CustomPersonaModal.tsx ~:106) + a short helper/counter if the pattern fits. Pure frontend, trivial. Not blocking -- the render is already clean without it.

items.title: Round-setup caddie is persona-blind in both voice and character
items.why: CADDIE-EXPERIENCE dim 8. VoiceRoundSetupRealtime.tsx:149,169 hardcode personalityId:'classic', and build_setup_instructions() (setup_voice.py:70-94) contains NO persona character at all (a generic 'You are Looper's caddie... like a friend on the first tee'). So even if the frontend sent the real persona, setup would only vary voice_id, never character (strategist's clipped numbers, hype's energy, professor's teaching all vanish during setup). The FIRST voice interaction of every round is a different caddie than the one the user configured. ACTION: pass personaId through and blend persona character into build_setup_instructions -- OR make a deliberate product decision that setup is always neutral and make that consistent everywhere.

items.title: CaddieSheet classic current-turn user question stays mono/small, not the shared serif-italic idiom

Not blocking — the rest of the surface already reads coherent.

items.title: Should a literal close->reopen of the converse orb sheet preserve conversation continuity?
items.why: Surfaced by the cycle-133 builder while writing the no-re-greet test: the designer concept assumed 'turns persist across close->reopen (safe today)' but that is FALSE — CaddieOrbSheet's setOpen((wasOpen)=>{ if(!wasOpen) resetSession(); return true; }) intentionally clears turns on every closed->open transition (pre-existing, unrelated to this cycle). So the converse orb starts a FRESH conversation on every reopen, whereas the round-side sheet preserves its thread. The builder correctly did NOT change this reset behavior (a real session-persistence change, out of scope for a copy-only nit) and re-scoped the test to the true invariant (continuity across a re-summon while the sheet STAYS open; the emptyHint never re-greets once turns.length>0). Reviewer + designer both flagged the underlying UX question as a fair product call for later. ACTION: owner decides whether converse close->reopen should preserve conversation continuity (matching round) or intentionally start fresh; if preserve, it's a small state-lifetime change to resetSession's trigger. UPDATE 2026-07-16: the IN-ROUND case is now SATISFIED by the untethered-caddie work (bundle #142, Item B) -- live-session ownership was lifted from CaddieSheet to RoundPageClient (useDetachedCaddieLive), so closing/reopening the round CaddieSheet preserves the running session + transcript. REMAINING for this item: only the off-course CONVERSE orb (CaddieOrbSheet.resetSession on closed->open) -- still needs-owner-decision whether it should preserve or start fresh.

items.title: Follow-up: complete the deferred money-settling game formats (wolf true-transfer, opponent/team pickers)
items.why: Deferred from cycle 100 (tournament-settlement-honesty, @2e17261 on integration/next / PR #135). That cycle made the picker HONEST by only offering a stake for formats that truly settle zero-sum (STAKE_GAME_IDS = {skins, match, nassau}) and PREVENTING silently-truncated games. Three capabilities were intentionally deferred as product/design-shaped, each a real feature the owner may want later: (1) WOLF as a money game -- computeWolf (games.ts ~836-862) is NOT zero-sum today: lone-mode credits the wolf +/-3 and debits no one; partner-mode awards +1/+1 to the winning pair and debits no one (reviewer falsified: a lone-wolf win netted +$6, sum != 0). To re-enable wolf stakes, make the deltas TRUE transfers (lone win: wolf +3, each other -1; lone loss: wolf -3, each other +1; partner: distribute the pot across the losing side -- a real rules decision) then re-add 'wolf' to SETTLEABLE_FORMATS + STAKE_GAME_IDS and add a decided-multi-hole zero-sum test (the new SETTLEABLE_FORMATS property test in settlement.test.ts will enforce it). (2) MATCH-PLAY opponent picker -- match play is restricted to exactly-2 rosters (prevents silent drop of players 3+); a >2 roster needs an opponent-selection UI writing game.settings.matchPlayPlayers (engine already supports it, games.ts:740-741). (3) TEAM-ASSIGNMENT UI -- unlocks stakes for the team formats currently non-settling-by-construction in the picker (vegas is team-only/unconstructible today; bestBall/scramble/threePoint need teams). Each is its own plan+tests; keep [[no-fake-data-fallbacks]]: never re-offer a stake until the format settles zero-sum end-to-end.
items.resolution: PART 1 (WOLF) SHIPPED TO MAIN 2026-07-12 (cycle 110, @741e6ac, bundle #137 merged @c715d2e; owner 'Ship it'; TestFlight v1.1.5 build 202607121234). Also in this bundle: threePoint zero-sum rounding-drift fix (cycle 112, @1ee8427, tournament settlement coverage hardening — real odd-cent-stake money bug). Option A per specs/tournament-wolf-settlement-plan.md (fable): computeWolf now emits a TRUE zero-sum per-hole pointsDelta (lone win wolf +3 / each of 3 opponents -1; lone loss mirror; partner win winners +1 / losers -1; partner loss mirror; ties and any incomplete-data hole -- missing pick, missing wolf score, or fewer than all 3 opponent scores in lone mode -- emit an EMPTY delta / $0), and settlement.ts settles money = totals x pointValue and re-adds 'wolf' to SETTLEABLE_FORMATS (STAKE_GAME_IDS derives it, so buildRoundGames carries wolf's stake again). Zero-sum enforced every hole; wolf registered in the SETTLEABLE_FORMATS property-test fixture (asymmetric p1 +$12 / p2 -$4 / p3 -$8 / p4 $0 at $2) which now passes. Data model already captured the pick (types.ts wolfHoleChoices/wolfOrderPlayerIds + in-round GameResults.tsx UI) -- NO type/backend/migration change. Scoreboard now shows negative point totals for hole losers (T.errorInk, matching Hammer/BBB) across GameResults/GameLeaderboards/LeaderboardSheet. reviewer SHIP (hand-recomputed fixture, verified every branch balances), qa PASS (vitest 2336/build/voice 278/ruff; CI backend SUCCESS), designer BLOCKING->APPROVE after the negative-total color/sign fix. Commits 9ba12db (engine+tests) + 741e6ac (designer fix + reviewer hardening). STILL OPEN (item stays ready): PART 2 match-play >2-player opponent picker and PART 3 team-assignment UI (vegas/bestBall/scramble/threePoint) -- both genuinely design/UX-gated, filed for an owner product decision; do NOT build until the owner decides how the golfer records opponents/teams.

items.title: Fetch real tee-time availability for EVERY course (API → scrape → AI-call ladder)
items.why: PLAN: specs/teetime-availability-everywhere-plan.md (Fable). KEY FINDING: Marine Park was NEVER phone-only — its site links a live EZLinks online-booking portal; the dead-end was a capability-DETECTION gap (we only know foreUP), not missing data. Ladder = 4 rungs: API → widget-XHR replay → headless render → AI-call → honest-empty. Sequenced S4a-S4f below. Most of the epic is engine-adapter breadth + per-course capability detection, NOT exotic scraping. OWNER DIRECTIVE 2026-07-10 (screenshot, Marine Park 'No online booking — call the pro shop'): showing a bare phone number is NOT good enough. Every course MUST return real availability in SOME form. Ladder: (1) clean booking-engine API where one exists (foreUP live; EVALUATE adding Chronogolf/Lightspeed/GolfNow-EZLinks/TeeItUp APIs), (2) WEB SCRAPE the rendered availability widget where no clean JSON API exists, (3) AI phone-call the pro shop (PR #124 caller) as the final fallback for phone-only courses. Owner explicitly OK with building a scraper if it's the best per-course option. PLAN FIRST on Fable (per fable-for-plan-agents): per-engine option eval, scraping feasibility+ToS/legal+infra (headless browser for JS-rendered widgets), caching (GolfAPI-style budget discipline), integration with the reachability router.

Unblock via voicetel-timing-immediate-flush, then read caddie-turn vs caddie-rt p90; <1.5-2s delta -> build hybrid, 3s+ -> stay put (vocab-bias+input-grounding already shipped may be enough). items.title: Spike: target-speaker filtering (voice enrollment + on-device TSVAD)
items.why: SPIKE DONE (PR #127): Tier-1 mic constraints (echoCancel/noiseSuppress/autoGain + backend near_field) ALREADY SHIPPED (~70% of the value, no code win left). Tier-2 TSVAD = FEASIBLE/medium as a NATIVE Core ML Capacitor plugin (WKWebView can't do threaded WASM); Personal-VAD arch, named pretrained models; pure tested SpeakerGate core committed unwired. GO when prioritized (sequence after voice-reliability). Tier-3 VoiceFilter = defer. OWNER 2026-07-09 (hands-free + only-owner's-voice): the real answer is a one-time voice ENROLLMENT (~5-10s -> speaker embedding) + a Target-Speaker VAD gate that opens the mic-to-model only when the enrolled owner speaks — rejects partners without breaking hands-free. NOT train-from-scratch (pretrained models exist). Full VoiceFilter extraction only if TSVAD gating insufficient. Fable plan. specs/voice-target-speaker-research.md.

items.why: SCOPED 2026-07-10 — NOT a clean bug, a DESIGN QUESTION (do not autonomously build). Header shows cardYards (scorecard hole length from the selected tee, RoundPageClient ~1194); Center tile shows fcbFromTee.center (straight-line tee→green geometry). On a from-tee non-live hole these differ by design — on a dogleg the card follows the fairway while geometry is straight-line, so a small 'drift' is EXPECTED and arguably correct. A 'clamp' that snaps the tile to the card would HIDE the real straight-line-to-green number (useful info). Needs owner/designer call: (a) is the drift actually confusing on-device (validate on a real TestFlight screenshot — the multi-tee anchor fix already resolved the phantom 178/232 case), (b) if so, prefer a caption/label clarification over clamping the number. Deferred pending that decision.

items.title: Bethpage 7/11 guide-validation: gap-class carry-SPAN fix SHIPPED (cycle 116, v1.1.6); two residual false-reject sub-classes filed
items.why: INVESTIGATION 2026-07-09 (cycle 39, OFFLINE from committed fixture tests/fixtures/bethpage_overpass.json — same data prod ingested; no prod SSM needed). Holes 7 & 11 were persistently honest-empty (research ~4x each fails validate_guide). ROOT CAUSE = (c) validator over-strict, NOT (a) geometry or (b) OSM. EVIDENCE: (a) ruled out — extract_hole_hazards gives correct sides/carries matching reality: hole 7 par-5 dogleg-right (polyline 346->36->55deg) bunkers R 170/430/520 + L 355/525; hole 11 par-4 bunkers both sides L 245/415 R 270/325/420. All normal/correct non-numbered guide phrasings PASS the validator. (b) ruled out on-hole — 7/11 have ONLY bunkers, nearest water 395-431y OFF the played line (not in play); a water/OB mention is therefore a CORRECT rejection (OSM has no 'ob' polygon type so any OB phrase always rejects — expected). (c) CONFIRMED + reproduced: _side_and_carry_supported() checks a claimed carry vs each bunker's CENTROID +-25y, but Bethpage bunkers are large — hole 7's right cross-bunker centroid=170y actually SPANS 103-280y (178y wide). A web-researched guide naturally citing any carry in the real footprint (220/240/250/260) but >25y from centroid -> WHOLE guide REJECTED (demonstrated: all four reject). That is why grounded, reasonable numbered advice keeps failing = the persistence pattern. DECISION NEEDED (genuine product/risk call, not an obvious fix): (c1) widen the carry check to the bunker's surveyed near->far carry SPAN (recovers guides on big-bunker holes) — but this loosens the grounding/anti-laundering control that had a bypass closed just days ago (5e4b861 carry-tie-break laundering), so it REQUIRES the full adversarial treatment: Fable plan + eval-harness teeth (each new accept-case proven RED pre-fix) + adversarial review proving no false number laundering re-opens; vs (c2) accept status quo — honest-empty is SAFE and correct (caddie falls back to the grounded generic hazard line); large-bunker holes simply get no cached prose guide. Recommend (c1) as a properly-reviewed future build item IF cached guides on such holes are wanted; otherwise close as working-as-designed. Optional confirmation: an on-box research pass capturing the exact rejected text would pin which number/phrase fired in prod, but the local reproduction already demonstrates the mechanism — not worth an unattended prod SSM action. FILES: backend/app/caddie/guide_writer.py (_side_and_carry_supported, _has_side_flip, _CARRY_TOLERANCE_YARDS=25); backend/app/caddie/hazards.py (extract_hole_hazards, centroid-based carry). FORBIDDEN to inline-fix per tasks/lessons.md (geometry/validation change without full adversarial treatment).
items.resolution: CYCLE 116 (2026-07-12): the carry-SPAN / contiguous-run fix (option c1, properly reviewed) LANDED in v1.1.6 (commit 5e4414b, PR #139; specs/guide-validator-carry-span-plan.md fable plan; reviewer opus SHIP after CONSTRUCTING a fabricated carry [BLACK-7 R{170,430,520} 'right bunker at 300'] and proving it still REJECTS; qa PASS ruff+137/137 targeted+2365 broad offline; backend-only). _side_and_carry_supported now accepts a claimed (type,side,carry) within [min-25,max+25] of a CONTIGUOUS RUN of that (type,side)'s sampled carries (bunker/water/ob bridge _CARRY_BRIDGE_YARDS=60; trees bridge unconditionally into one [min,max] run since the near/far pair brackets a continuous line). Strict SUPERSET of old accepts -> ZERO existing-guide regressions (Black 16 / Red 15 / Pebble 18 intact). On-box regen (sanctioned; fixed code from 5e4414b materialized to /tmp, app .env, LOOPER_SECRETS_DISABLED=1, claude-sonnet-5, one bounded pass) of the 5 negative-cached holes: 3/5 RECOVERED -> RED 1/8/18 now cached & grounded (Red 18/18; e.g. Red 8 'left bunkers at 160 and 195'=geom {160,195,365}). BLACK 7/11 re-rejected -> honest-empty (Black still 16/18). The on-box diagnostic REVISED the cycle-39 footprint theory: (i) BLACK 11 = a DIFFERENT, pre-existing false-reject in _has_side_flip NUMBER-BINDING (untouched here) -- the both-sides sentence 'the 245-left bunker and the 270/325 right-side bunkers' binds right-side 325 to the nearby LEFT bunker keyword's window and rejects, though every number is correctly grounded to its true side; (ii) BLACK 7 = STOCHASTIC (a fresh diagnostic candidate PASSED; the regen candidate cited an ungrounded number) -- left honest-empty, no retry storm. The single-large-bunker-footprint case was NOT reproduced this pass (lower confidence than the cycle-39 write-up). Two follow-ups filed: guide-validator-cross-side-binding (the observed BLACK 11 cause) + guide-validator-bunker-footprint-span (the cycle-39 theory). Honest-empty stays SAFE (generic grounded hazard line). CYCLE 118 (2026-07-13): the cross-SIDE binding sub-class is FIXED + proven (commit 12cec56, see guide-validator-cross-side-binding=done; a fresh real Black-11 both-sides candidate now VALIDATES PASS on prod geometry). Black 7/11 still honest-empty (Black 16/18) because a THIRD residual surfaced on-box -- cross-TYPE number pollution (a trees carry adjacent to a bunker keyword, validated vs bunker geometry) filed as guide-validator-cross-type-number-binding -- plus Black-7 play_line verbosity (397>240). One of the two cycle-116 sub-classes now closed; two residuals (footprint-span [low-confidence], cross-type-number-binding [observed]) remain.

items.title: Guide validator: a single LARGE bunker is stored as ONE centroid -> a legit carry within its footprint but >25y off centroid is rejected (per-bunker front/back-edge extraction)
items.why: Cycle-39 theory (a big single bunker's centroid vs its 100-180y-wide footprint; e.g. Black 7 right cross-bunker centroid 170 spanning ~103-280) for BLACK 7/11 honest-empty. Cycle 116's carry-SPAN fix bridges MULTIPLE bunker centroids of one (type,side) within 60y (this recovered RED 1/8/18) but does NOT widen a SINGLE bunker's centroid to its footprint span. CAVEAT: cycle 116's on-box diagnostic did NOT reproduce a footprint-class reject on BLACK 7 this pass (that candidate passed; the observed Black 11 reject was the cross-side binding issue) -- so this class is now LOWER-confidence than the cycle-39 write-up implied. If cached guides on such holes are wanted: extract per-bunker near->far carry (front/back edge) in hazards.py (currently centroid-only) so _side_and_carry_supported can accept a carry within a single bunker's real footprint. Bigger change (hazards.py extraction) needing full adversarial treatment. FILES: backend/app/caddie/hazards.py (extract_hole_hazards centroid-based carry), backend/app/caddie/guide_writer.py. FORBIDDEN to inline-fix per tasks/lessons.md.

items.title: Per-user voice language setting (onboarding + profile)
items.why: Owner 2026-07-06: caddie transcribed him in Korean (auto-detect). Transcription is now PINNED to English (realtime_relay.py) as the default; when onboarding exists, add a language picker (profile-stored, feeds the transcription language + instructions) instead of the hardcode. UPDATE 2026-07-16 (owner escalation -> HARD English-output contract landed on bundle #142): the pin is now a single seam backend/app/caddie/language.py::desired_language() (returns ('en','English')) feeding ALL mouths -- realtime instructions (output_language_rule, defensive against ambient/overheard non-English + explicit switch requests), both routes/caddie.py text stable_text blocks, TTS (implicit-from-text, covered), and the input transcription pin. This onboarding item now changes ONLY that one function (return the profile-selected language); no call sites move.

Candidate — needs a short spec + owner nod on which personas/voices.

items.title: S4d: rung-2b headless scraper (Playwright worker) + 'checking…' pending UX
items.why: DEFERRED per Fable verdict (specs/teetime-headless-scraper-plan.md §7): NO bucket-(c) target in NY-metro — every remaining engine is httpx-addable (CPS/Quick18/Teesnap) or anti-bot-walled (EZLinks→rung3, browser can't pass Turnstile politely). ~400MB Chromium would fetch ZERO extra courses = headless-for-its-own-sake. Fully specced+gated; reopen ONLY if the coverage flywheel finds a JS-render-gated, non-anti-bot engine with no replayable XHR. | NOTE 2026-07-10: EZLinks/Cloudflare-Turnstile sites are NOT scrapable politely even with a headless browser (probe confirmed Chromium never clears Turnstile) — do NOT target them with S4d. S4d headless is only for JS-rendered widgets WITHOUT anti-bot gates. Re-scope S4d's target engine after S4a/S4c reveal which engines actually need a browser. The only slice with deploy-image impact (~400MB Chromium, gated TEETIME_SCRAPER_ENABLED default off). In-process asyncio worker + bounded queue off the request path; TeeTimeSlot gains status live|pending; poll endpoint; frontend pending row; first browser-scraped engine (Club Prophet or hostile EZLinks). Plan §4b,§6,§7. Depends on S4a.

items.title: True per-course handicap adjustment for tournaments with different courses per round
items.why: specs/tournament-per-round-format-course-plan.md §8 (per-round COURSE plan, cycle 120): standings/settlement are deliberately course-blind — computeStandings applies one manually-set handicap per player to every round regardless of which course it was played on. A true course-handicap adjustment (course rating/slope → different course handicap at Bethpage Black vs Bethpage Red) needs rating/slope data the app doesn't store today. Honest follow-up, NOT built as part of the course-plan feature — do not silently "adjust" net scores without real rating data.

items.title: DECISION: enable GPS nearest-green auto-advance on the round page, or keep swipe/tap-only
items.why: specs/oncourse-gps-readiness-plan.md (owner's Bethpage Red readiness pass, 2026-07-16), behavior #4 of the GPS-consumer audit: auto-advance-to-next-hole-on-walk logic already exists in GoogleSatelliteMap.tsx handlePositionUpdate (948-958) but is disabled by design — autoDetectHole={false} at both InlineHoleDiagram.tsx:306 and RoundPageClient.tsx:2658 — so today holes change only by swipe/tap. NEEDS AN OWNER DECISION before building: enabling nearest-green auto-detect risks mis-jumping between adjacent greens on a tight routing (e.g. holes whose greens sit close together, common at classic A.W. Tillinghast-era designs like Bethpage). Not changed this pass — the map-lane file (GoogleSatelliteMap.tsx) is out of scope for the readiness harness.

items.title: Tune tree penalty so very-tight tree corridors lay a high-handicap player back (fable-review non-blocker follow-up)
items.why: Non-blocking observation from the fable adversarial re-review of caddie-tee-club-expected-strokes (2026-07-18, verdict SHIP): with the trees=0.7 base penalty (now handicap-scaled), a 30-handicap player is still recommended driver on a 20y-wide tree corridor where the Gaussian model puts ~72% of drives in the trees. The handicap-scaling made this STRICTLY more cautious than before the P0 fix (so it is not a regression and was correctly left out of that delta's scope), but a ~72%-trees driver for a wild hitter on a genuinely narrow tree chute is arguably still too aggressive. This is a calibration question (the trees severity constant / whether tree cost should grow super-linearly with dispersion width, or whether an absolute P(trouble) ceiling should cap the aggressive pick), NOT a structural bug — the expected-strokes machinery is sound. Revisit only if the owner reports over-aggression on tight tree holes for higher handicaps; pin any change with a hcp-30 / width-20 tree fixture test. Also noted: the long-bag water-pinch layup margin is thin (E-gap 0.025 vs the 0.02 tie tolerance) — pinned by test_05b, correct today, but a small recalibration could flip it in direction only that test guards; keep the regression.

items.title: OPS/owner: enable Google + Apple SSO connections + Native Applications on the prod Clerk instance
items.why: Prereq (owner/ops action, not app code) for the login-onboarding redesign epic (specs/login-onboarding-redesign-plan.md §2.5). The headless custom login screen renders Google/Apple/email buttons, but Google + Apple must be enabled as SSO connections on the PRODUCTION Clerk instance (Dashboard -> Configure -> SSO connections), and Native Applications must be on (already referenced by the AuthProvider native-token bridge). App Store Guideline 4.8 makes Apple sign-in mandatory once Google is offered. Blocked on the owner doing the Dashboard config; the auth-headless-spike can dev against a dev instance meanwhile but ships only once prod connections are live.

This supersedes the earlier 'no bottom tab' recommendation. Build it in the yardage-book aesthetic (on-paper, restrained, calm — not generic SaaS) and HIDE it on immersive screens (active round / voice / GPS) where the app should disappear. Likely tabs: Round/Home · Partners · Courses (+ profile). This is the 'minimal home dock' path from the plan, now promoted to the chosen approach + extended to a persistent floating tab. A new card 'nav-floating-island-tab' tracks the build.
